message.content: While gravity pulls harder on the heavier ball, that ball also has more inertia, which means it has a greater resistance to changing its motion. These two factors cancel each other out perfectly, causing both balls to accelerate at the exact same rate of 9.8 meters per second squared. You must now explain in your own words how inertia and gravitational force balance each other out to keep the acceleration of both balls equal.

message.content: Newton's Second Law of Motion is the law you need, which is written as the formula $a = F/m$, meaning acceleration equals force divided by mass. Since the heavier ball has twice the gravitational force acting on it but also twice the mass, the ratio of force to mass remains exactly the same for both objects. You must now mathematically demonstrate how doubling both the force ($F$) and the mass ($m$) in this formula results in the exact same acceleration ($a$).
